draft_picks.reasoning: Suns 39-27 (better season record than Toronto 36-29) and in excellent recent form (4W-1L last five games). Phoenix travelling to Toronto but carry the superior season record — market has priced them at ~42% implied win probability. Scottie Barnes (Toronto's best player and franchise cornerstone) is listed QUESTIONABLE with illness; if he misses the game the Suns' true win probability rises materially above 42%, making 2.37 strongly +EV. Even if Barnes plays, the value at 2.37 is marginal positive. Devin Booker leads a balanced Suns attack. Suns averaged +8.6 ppg over last 5 games. Toronto has been inconsistent on home court in March. Road underdog market often undervalues teams with the better record.

draft_picks.match: Houston Rockets vs New Orleans Pelicans
draft_picks.reasoning: Houston at home (-7.5) vs New Orleans Pelicans (tanking, bottom-5 NBA record ~21-45). Kevin Durant confirmed available for Houston (featured in Betano scoring proposition bets for tonight). Pelicans are deep in lottery positioning and have strong incentive NOT to win — roster motivation severely diminished. Houston's Toyota Center is a tough road environment. Pelicans allow 120+ OPPG (27th in league), making it easy for a full-strength Rockets team to build a large lead. At 1.82 for a -7.5 line against a tanking opponent with Durant available, this represents solid spread value. Houston ML priced at 1.28 (implied 78% win probability) — backing them to cover against a depleted, unmotivated team.

settled_bets.reasoning: Heat near even money at 1.98 vs Pistons — historically resilient franchise under Spoelstra; competitive matchup for both teams fighting for playoff position
Kings at even money (2.00) offers excellent value; De'Aaron Fox and Domantas Sabonis-led offense capable of winning at home; road Bulls at 1.70 seems overpriced
Suns at 2.42 looks undervalued for a talent-rich roster; speculative play on market overreaction to recent Suns struggles; home court advantage

76ers are catastrophically undermanned: Embiid OUT (oblique), Paul George OUT (suspension), Maxey OUT (finger) — their top 3 players all absent. Cavaliers have Mitchell back from injury and are at near full strength. Home court advantage. Spread opened at -10.5, moved to -12.5 on injury news. At 1.91 for -12.5, market still offers good value for a decimated opponent.

settled_bets.match: Oklahoma City Thunder vs Denver Nuggets
settled_bets.reasoning: OKC is severely depleted: Jalen Williams OUT (hamstring), Isaiah Hartenstein OUT (calf), Alex Caruso QUESTIONABLE (hip), Chet Holmgren QUESTIONABLE (illness). Jokic confirmed playing despite minute restriction. Getting +7.5 against a potentially 4-man-down OKC with Jokic available is excellent spread value. Jamal Murray confirmed available at tip-off.

Michael Porter Jr. confirmed OUT for the Nets (their leading scorer). Grizzlies are the more complete team even without Ja Morant — better depth, better coaching, better defensive intensity. Nets sitting near bottom of East with no playoff ambition — potential tanking risk. Value at 1.75 for the superior roster.

settled_bets.match: San Antonio Spurs vs Boston Celtics
settled_bets.reasoning: Spurs are 47-17, best record in the NBA — won 15 of last 16, 12-4 ATS in last 16 games. Celtics missing Vucevic (OUT, finger surgery) and Tatum limited to ~27-min restriction (only 3rd game back from Achilles tear). Victor Wembanyama dominant at home — Spurs just demolished Houston 145-120. Depleted Celtics frontcourt vs Wembanyama spells trouble covering the spread. Spurs home court (Frost Bank Center) — commanding 33-6 home record this season.

settled_bets.match: Los Angeles Lakers vs Minnesota Timberwolves
settled_bets.reasoning: LeBron James confirmed OUT for 3rd straight game (left foot arthritis + hip contusion). Luka Doncic (32.5 ppg, #1 scorer in NBA) at home on NBC primetime — capable of carrying the Lakers on a big-stage night. Lakers home court at Crypto.com Arena — crowd factor in a marquee TV game. Market prices Lakers at ~49% implied probability (2.05); marginal value given Luka's ceiling at home.

settled_bets.match: Chicago Bulls vs Golden State Warriors
settled_bets.reasoning: Warriors are on the second leg of a back-to-back (played Jazz in Utah on Monday, 32+ min from key players). Draymond Green listed as QUESTIONABLE (injury management, lower back). Bulls arrive rested and already acclimated to the West Coast. Line has barely moved suggesting market is not fully pricing in Warriors fatigue. Getting +6.5 on a rested team against a fatigued, depleted opponent is sharp value.

settled_bets.match: Los Angeles Clippers vs Minnesota Timberwolves
settled_bets.reasoning: Timberwolves on second night of back-to-back — lost to Lakers 106-120 on March 10; Anthony Edwards shot 2-for-15 (14 pts), heavy fatigue carry-over. Clippers at home (Intuit Dome) on three-game home winning streak. Kawhi Leonard averaging 27.9 ppg. Road B2B historically depresses winning %. Odds improved from 1.70 to 1.74 at commit — good sign. T-wolves 40-25 but road B2B is real handicap.

settled_bets.match: New Orleans Pelicans vs Toronto Raptors
settled_bets.reasoning: Late scout flip: Raptors are 36-28 (5th East, playoff team) vs Pelicans 21-45 (tanking). Road team favoured at -132 (1.76 decimal) because Raptors have the 4th-best road record in the NBA at 19-11. Pelicans allow 120.2 OPPG (27th in league). Original 14:00 pick of Pelicans ML was an error — line moved >0.22 against that pick, market correctly repriced. Poeltl questionable for Raptors is minor risk but talent/record gap is too large to bet the Pelicans. Value at 1.76 for a playoff team on road.

settled_bets.match: San Antonio Spurs vs Denver Nuggets
settled_bets.reasoning: Wembanyama listed as QUESTIONABLE (sore right ankle, true 50/50 game-time decision) — market still pricing Denver at 2.50 before final Wemby status. Jokic not on injury report (Denver beat Houston in dominant fashion Wednesday night), Jamal Murray PROBABLE (ankle, expected to play). If Wemby sits, Denver's implied win probability rises to ~55-60% making 2.50 massively +EV. Combined EV across both Wemby scenarios is strongly positive. Harrison Barnes also QUESTIONABLE for SA. Denver 40-26, San Antonio 48-17 — talent gap narrows significantly without Wembanyama.

settled_bets.match: Oklahoma City Thunder vs Boston Celtics
settled_bets.reasoning: Boston's Jayson Tatum is OUT (reconditioning — first miss since Achilles return) and Nikola Vucevic OUT (hand surgery), Derrick White QUESTIONABLE. Boston (51-15, best record in NBA) is severely depleted for this road game. OKC has Chet Holmgren and Alex Caruso both back from injury; only Jalen Williams remains OUT. OKC near full strength at home. Even the best team in the NBA loses a significant chunk of win probability without their best player on a road game.
